Lavanya Gandhi
Analyst, Strategy, Growth & Transformation · Consulting · Deloitte USI (SCNO)

Strategy, Growth & Transformation Analyst at Deloitte USI's Supply Chain & Network Operations practice. I work on large-scale network transformation, vendor strategy, program governance, and data-driven solutions for global enterprises. I hold a Master's in Economics degree from Gokhale Institute of Politics and Economics, Pune, and I'm fascinated by the intersection of economics, technology, and human behavior — how data and behavioral insights can shape smarter business strategies.

Beyond the numbers and analytics, I bring sharp analytical thinking, creative problem-solving, and a collaborative spirit. I thrive in dynamic environments where innovative ideas are encouraged and diverse teams come together to drive meaningful impact. My current engagement involves consolidating a global hardware-tech client's North American distribution network from 22 centers into 4 regional mega-sites — spanning vendor strategy, request for proposal (RFP) analysis, program management across 1,900+ activities, and go-live execution.

Deloitte USI › HP Engagement
01.1 /Deloitte USI (SCNO) · HP engagement

HP Greenfield Network Transformation Program.

Aug 2024 — Present

A large-scale greenfield program transforming HP's North America logistics network, consolidating 22 distribution centers (DCs) into 4 regional mega-sites (East, Central, West, Canada) to achieve 2-day delivery coverage and operational resilience, managed end-to-end by a third-party logistics (3PL) vendor. Targeting under 5-day order-to-delivery, over 80% sales-order line redundancy, and substantial cost savings.

Phase 1NA Greenfield
Aug 2024
delivered
Vendor strategy, RFP & network design (East & Central sites)
  • Designed the end-to-end request for information (RFI), RFP, and vendor scoring methodology; conducted market analysis across 16 vendors, shortlisting to 6 qualified 3PL partners on capability, coverage, and strategic fit
  • Translated 3 business requirements documents (BRDs) into 240 granular business requirements, and evaluated 23 Power BI data files to size site volumes, establishing the functional baseline for vendor evaluation
  • Analysed 145 vendor response documents across 21 parameters, and validated costing files from the 6 shortlisted vendors, identifying overstatement and concrete negotiation levers for HP
  • Served as sole USI resource for around a month during a critical transition, independently managing 4 key deliverables and mentoring a junior consultant, recognised by leadership for high-quality output and client value
  • Structured the ISOW and OSOW contract frameworks and facilitated vendor and internal kickoffs, aligning 9 workstreams and establishing program governance
  • Developed and managed the Smartsheet program plan, with automated project management notifications across milestones and sign-off gates for the East and Central sites
  • Awarded 3PL vendors for the East and Central mega-sites on schedule, following RFP evaluation, financial validation, and stakeholder alignment; recognised by HP as a trusted resource, contributing to $3M in follow-on sales as scope expanded from 3 to 4 mega-sites
Phase 2NA Greenfield
2025 — 2026
delivered
Scope expansion, go-live & performance governance (West & Canada sites)
  • Led the vendor award process for West and Canada, completing 3PL selection for all 4 sites within program timelines as scope expanded from 3 to 4 mega-sites, contributing to $3M in follow-on sales
  • Spearheaded the Smartsheet program plan tracking 1,900+ activities across 5 phases and 9 workstreams, enabling real-time progress, risk reporting, and escalation
  • Orchestrated PMO governance: mapped 32 statement of work (SOW) documents to close scope gaps, and built a cross-functional key design decision (KDD) and SIA tracker (23 scope items) to ensure contractual alignment
  • Facilitated design workshops tracking 500+ action items, and delivered a quality assurance (QA) dashboard monitoring 7,000 testing scenarios for real-time reporting across all workstreams
  • Evaluated 6 location scenarios across 3 configurations, contributing directly to $100M in savings by FY27 and a 5% service-level improvement in the Western region; $4M realized to date
  • Led the East mega-site go-live (Apr 2026), the first of 4 planned, overseeing golden transactions and post go-live service-level agreement (SLA) and key performance indicator (KPI) tracking to ensure 3PL performance met operational standards

Deloitte USI › HP Engagement › Agentic AI
01.3 /Deloitte USI · Agentic AI within the HP engagement

Agentic AI solution development.

Within the HP engagement, I independently built 2 agentic-AI solutions serving two distinct purposes. The PMO Agent came from a repeatable, high-effort project management office (PMO) process I identified internally and automated to cut reporting effort by 85%. The KPI Exception Agent was built to meet a client need, giving HP a way to monitor their key performance indicators (KPIs) and track service-level agreement (SLA) compliance across their 3PL and 4PL vendors in real time.

pmo.agent
LIVE

PMO Agent

// weekly executive summary & action tracking across 9 workstreams

Running the weekly PMO cycle, synthesizing updates from 9 workstreams, tracking action items from the driving-team meetings held twice a week, and producing the executive summary, I built this agent to automate the workflow end to end, cutting my weekly reporting effort from 4 hours to 35 minutes (an 85% reduction). It handles two core functions:

Driving Team Action Items — Parses notes from the driving-team meetings held twice a week and extracts structured action items, assigning an owner and due date to each. The agent then sorts by date, classifying items as high-priority, completed, or overdue, so the items needing immediate attention surface first.

Weekly Workstream Synthesis — Gathers the weekly updates from 9 cross-functional workstreams and consolidates them into a single program-level view that gives leadership oversight across accomplishments, upcoming work, milestones, risks, and where help is needed.

Intake
Ingests driving-team meeting notes and the weekly updates from across the 9 workstreams.
Parse & extract
Converts meeting notes into structured action items with owner, due date, and priority classification.
Gather
Collects the weekly updates submitted across the 9 workstreams.
Synthesise
Consolidates all data into a program-level view: accomplishments, upcoming work, milestones, risks, per-workstream status, and help flags.
Deliver
Outputs an executive-ready summary for program leadership and stakeholders.
kpi_exception.agent
LIVE

KPI Exception Agent  ·  Control Tower  (ongoing)

// real-time KPI & SLA monitoring across 3PL vendor sites

Built within the HP engagement to help HP monitor their key performance indicators (KPIs) across sites, track service-level agreement (SLA) compliance across their 3PL vendors, identify exceptions in real time, and act on recommendations. The system consolidates network performance into five core functions:

Setup — Configures company details, sites, KPIs by workstream, role-based ownership, and business and maintenance hours, establishing the governance baseline before launch.

Overview — Surfaces SLA compliance status, site health, a geographic map of all sites, and a performance percentage for each distribution center.

Dashboard — Drills into performance by workstream with filters for workstream, site, and time period, showing top-line KPIs, status counts (green, at-risk, breaching), and detailed KPI lists per site.

Exceptions — Lists every triggered exception with owner, acknowledgement status, severity, and stage; each opens to root cause analysis, activity history, and options to acknowledge, escalate, or resolve.

Recommendations — Provides one-off recommendations for individual exceptions, and pattern-analysis recommendations that flag recurring exceptions across sites and suggest preventive measures.

Ingest
Consolidates KPI and SLA data across all sites and 3PL vendor performance metrics.
Monitor
Tracks KPI status and SLA compliance against defined thresholds in real time.
Flag
Surfaces exceptions as they occur, with severity and role-based owner assignment.
Analyse
Generates root cause analysis and detects recurring patterns across the network.
Recommend
Provides recommendations for immediate resolution and future prevention.
Deloitte USI › Firm Contributions
01.4 /Deloitte USI · Firm contributions & other engagements

Firm contributions & other engagements.

Assets and engagements delivered at Deloitte USI beyond the HP program.

01

WMS/TMS Vendor Evaluation Accelerator

Developed a standardised RFP package, site-visit templates, and cross-industry scoring methodology for 3PL/4PL and WMS vendors, integrating 300+ business requirements synthesised from 4 distinct client engagements. Published on the firm's knowledge platform (KX) and adopted by 11 projects firm-wide, projected to reduce project delivery time by around 30%.

300+ requirements11 projectsPublished on KX
02

PVH Network Optimization, RFP & Spend Analysis

Scoped a network optimization initiative using a geocoded master file and Power BI visualizations across 40 nodes, identifying coverage gaps and strategic opportunities across the distribution network. Built a spend-category dashboard analysing $64M in transportation data to drive executive storyboarding and insight generation for leadership presentations.

$64M analysed40 nodes
03

SCNO USI Leadership Profile Book

Curated and published profiles for ~400 SCNO USI leaders in partnership with 35 managers and 50+ senior consultants, producing a go-to staffing and networking reference. Achieved ~2,200 views in 5 months, improving staffing efficiency for practitioners rolling off engagements and helping new joiners connect with leaders in their areas of interest.

~400 leaders~2,200 views
